analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 9 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: apb_soc_ctrl updates SoC boot, fetch, cluster reset/power/bypass, mux, JTAG output, and pad-control state from APB writes gated only by PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE and register address decode; no privilege, lifecycle, lock, or debug-authorization guard is evident in the provided RTL observations.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/trigger boot and execution-control state / fc_bootaddr_o, fc_fetchen_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, sel_hyper_axi_o, soc_jtag_reg_o, pad_cfg/pad_mux / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB/peripheral bus requester reaching s_soc_ctrl_bus through periph_bus_wrap Operation: write/configure/trigger boot and execution-control state Object: fc_bootaddr_o, fc_fetchen_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, sel_hyper_axi_o, soc_jtag_reg_o, pad_cfg/pad_mux Expected guard: explicit privilege/lock/lifecycle/debug-authorization condition in addition to APB handshake and address decode Observed behavior: apb_soc_ctrl defines s_apb_write = PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE, decodes PADDR[8:2], resets sensitive registers to defaults including r_bootaddr=32'h1A000000 and r_jtag_rego=0, and drives protected outputs directly from internal registers. Tool observations show pad_cfg writes under register cases and no guard beyond APB write/address decode in the visible write path. Path: shared APB slave -> periph_bus_wrap -> s_soc_ctrl_bus -> apb_soc_ctrl register file Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Any master that can issue APB writes to the SoC-control address window can alter boot/fetch/reset/power/bypass/mux/JTAG-visible control state, potentially changing execution entry, releasing/resetting domains, powering/bypassing cluster logic, or exposing/modifying JTAG-facing state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full apb_soc_ctrl write-case body was truncated, so each individual protected register assignment is not fully enumerated. However the visible common write enable and output assignments show no authorization guard. Top-level APB master trust model is not specified.

findings.summary: The fact layer identifies s_apb_debug_bus, s_lint_debug_bus, s_lint_jtag_bus, and lint_jtag_wrap instance i_lint_jtag at top level. apb_soc_ctrl has soc_jtag_reg_i and soc_jtag_reg_o ports, with soc_jtag_reg_o driven from r_jtag_rego. A search for 'auth' under ips/pulp_soc/rtl found no authorization signal naming except comments. periph_bus_wrap includes a mmap_debug_master output for DEBUG_START_ADDR..DEBUG_END_ADDR.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/expose/override through debug or JTAG path / soc_jtag_reg_i/o, APB debug bus, lint_jtag/debug paths, memory/peripheral targets reachable through interconnect / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug interface requester Operation: read/write/expose/override through debug or JTAG path Object: soc_jtag_reg_i/o, APB debug bus, lint_jtag/debug paths, memory/peripheral targets reachable through interconnect Expected guard: debug authorization/lifecycle enable for debug/JTAG access and no bypass of normal APB/interconnect access mediation Observed behavior: The fact layer identifies s_apb_debug_bus, s_lint_debug_bus, s_lint_jtag_bus, and lint_jtag_wrap instance i_lint_jtag at top level. apb_soc_ctrl has soc_jtag_reg_i and soc_jtag_reg_o ports, with soc_jtag_reg_o driven from r_jtag_rego. A search for 'auth' under ips/pulp_soc/rtl found no authorization signal naming except comments. periph_bus_wrap includes a mmap_debug_master output for DEBUG_START_ADDR..DEBUG_END_ADDR. Path: s_apb_debug_bus, s_lint_debug_bus, s_lint_jtag_bus, i_lint_jtag, and soc_jtag_reg_i/o connectivity Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If debug/JTAG paths can reach SoC control or memory/peripheral targets without lifecycle authorization, they may read/write protected state. Provided observations establish presence of debug/JTAG buses but not the complete authorization or target reachability semantics.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: lint_jtag_wrap internals and complete top-level debug connectivity were not provided. The observations do not prove that debug/JTAG can reach soc_ctrl or memory targets, nor do they show lifecycle/debug enable signals other than absence of 'auth' naming.

findings.summary: apb_fll_if exposes direct APB write accesses to FLL1/FLL2/FLL3 request channels without using fll1_lock/fll2_lock/fll3_lock to block writes; lock bits are synchronized and readable but not included in the write-access conditions shown.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure FLL registers / fll1_add/data/wrn/req, fll2_add/data/wrn/req, fll3_add/data/wrn/req and associated FLL configuration state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester via s_fll_bus Operation: write/configure FLL registers Object: fll1_add/data/wrn/req, fll2_add/data/wrn/req, fll3_add/data/wrn/req and associated FLL configuration state Expected guard: corresponding fll*_lock asserted should block/restrict writes and prevent alternate write request generation Observed behavior: Write decode under PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE sets fll1_wr_access/fll2_wr_access/fll3_wr_access based only on PADDR[5:2]. The fll*_wrn/add/data outputs depend on fll*_valid and PWRITE/PADDR/PWDATA. The visible read logic exposes lock state at address 4'b1111, but write logic does not reference fll*_lock_sync. soc_peripherals connects fll*_lock to FLL master lock outputs from soc_clk_rst_gen. Path: shared APB -> s_fll_bus -> apb_fll_if -> soc/per/cluster FLL master interfaces Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: APB software can issue writes to FLL configuration channels even when the target FLL reports lock asserted, potentially modifying clock-generation configuration that should be lock-protected.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The FSM section generating fll*_valid/fll*_req was partially truncated in initial observations, but the provided complete write/read output section shows no lock use in write decode or command data generation.

findings.summary: Peripheral APB range definitions contain overlapping address windows: GPIO covers 0x1A10_1000..0x1A10_AFFF while UDMA covers 0x1A10_2000..0x1A10_4FFF, SOC_CTRL covers 0x1A10_4000..0x1A10_4FFF, ADV_TIMER covers 0x1A10_5000..0x1A10_5FFF, SOC_EVENT_GEN covers 0x1A10_6000..0x1A10_6FFF, and EU covers 0x1A10_9000..0x1A10_AFFF. apb_node independently asserts psel_o[i] for every matching range, so overlapping addresses can select multiple slaves.
findings.vulnerability_category: route APB read/write to peripheral slaves / s_fll_bus, s_gpio_bus, s_udma_bus, s_soc_ctrl_bus, s_adv_timer_bus, s_soc_evnt_gen_bus, s_stdout_bus, s_apb_timer_bus, debug/HWPE/EU APB masters / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: shared APB bus requester Operation: route APB read/write to peripheral slaves Object: s_fll_bus, s_gpio_bus, s_udma_bus, s_soc_ctrl_bus, s_adv_timer_bus, s_soc_evnt_gen_bus, s_stdout_bus, s_apb_timer_bus, debug/HWPE/EU APB masters Expected guard: exact non-overlapping address ranges or priority/error logic preventing aliases to protected peripherals Observed behavior: apb_node assigns psel_o[i] = (paddr_i >= START_ADDR_i[i]) && (paddr_i <= END_ADDR_i[i]) independently for each master. periph_bus_wrap maps the range constants from periph_bus_defines. The constants overlap substantially, including UDMA and SOC_CTRL sharing 0x1A10_4000..0x1A10_4FFF and GPIO covering most other peripheral windows. No exclusive decode or overlap error is shown in the provided apb_node excerpt. Path: apb_slave -> periph_bus_wrap -> apb_node_wrap -> apb_node psel_o generation -> per-slave APB buses Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A single APB address may drive PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE/PWDATA to multiple peripherals. For protected regions such as soc_ctrl and FLL/uDMA, overlapping decode can create aliases, unintended side effects, ambiguous PRDATA/PREADY return selection, or access to a protected slave through an apparently different window.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The truncated remainder of apb_node may contain return-data selection behavior, but independent overlapping psel generation is explicit. No evidence of overlap assertion/error or priority masking was provided.

findings.summary: The fact layer and top-level snippets show multiple local bus masters and slave targets. soc_bus_defines defines cluster, L2, L2 private/SCM alias, SoC APB, FC peripheral, FC APB, and stdout regions. soc_interconnect_wrap exposes lint_fc_data, lint_fc_instr, lint_udma_tx, lint_udma_rx, lint_debug, lint_hwpe, axi_from_cluster, axi_to_cluster, apb_periph_bus, L2/PRI/ROM memory buses. No explicit per-master authorization or protection matrix is visible in the provided excerpts.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/route memory and peripheral transactions / s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, peripheral/APB windows, L2/SCM memory regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: FC data/instruction, uDMA TX/RX, debug, JTAG local bus masters Operation: read/write/route memory and peripheral transactions Object: s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, peripheral/APB windows, L2/SCM memory regions Expected guard: consistent per-master address range checks or access matrix/PMP-like mediation Observed behavior: The fact layer and top-level snippets show multiple local bus masters and slave targets. soc_bus_defines defines cluster, L2, L2 private/SCM alias, SoC APB, FC peripheral, FC APB, and stdout regions. soc_interconnect_wrap exposes lint_fc_data, lint_fc_instr, lint_udma_tx, lint_udma_rx, lint_debug, lint_hwpe, axi_from_cluster, axi_to_cluster, apb_periph_bus, L2/PRI/ROM memory buses. No explicit per-master authorization or protection matrix is visible in the provided excerpts. Path: soc_interconnect_wrap/soc_interconnect from local and AXI masters to ROM/L2/peripheral APB Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If region decode lacks per-master restrictions, debug/JTAG/uDMA/HWPE paths may access memory/peripheral regions with the same privileges as the FC, but the available observations do not establish whether such unrestricted routing is implemented.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The actual decode/arbitration/routing logic in soc_interconnect and soc_interconnect_wrap was not provided beyond module headers and constants. No complete evidence for or against per-master range checks is available.

findings.summary: soc_peripherals instantiates udma_subsystem, connects APB-configured subsystem to s_udma_bus and exposes two L2 master interfaces: L2_ro_* to l2_tx_master and L2_wo_* to l2_rx_master. periph_bus_defines maps UDMA to 0x1A10_2000..0x1A10_4FFF, overlapping SOC_CTRL and GPIO. No uDMA channel register decode, bounds checks, or busy-update logic is present in the provided observations.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure DMA and issue memory reads/writes / uDMA RX/TX local interconnect buses and APB-configured DMA channel address/size/control state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester configuring uDMA; uDMA master issuing transfers Operation: configure DMA and issue memory reads/writes Object: uDMA RX/TX local interconnect buses and APB-configured DMA channel address/size/control state Expected guard: address range/access-policy checks and active-transfer update restrictions Observed behavior: soc_peripherals instantiates udma_subsystem, connects APB-configured subsystem to s_udma_bus and exposes two L2 master interfaces: L2_ro_* to l2_tx_master and L2_wo_* to l2_rx_master. periph_bus_defines maps UDMA to 0x1A10_2000..0x1A10_4FFF, overlapping SOC_CTRL and GPIO. No uDMA channel register decode, bounds checks, or busy-update logic is present in the provided observations. Path: s_udma_bus -> udma_subsystem APB configuration -> l2_tx_master/l2_rx_master local interconnect masters Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: uDMA can act as an interconnect master, so missing bounds could bypass CPU privilege; however the evidence does not show whether bounds or active-transfer controls exist.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: udma_subsystem internal channel configuration registers, address/size generation, range checks, busy semantics, and error behavior were not provided.

findings.summary: apb_gpio includes a GPIOLOCK register, but the observed write logic only applies lock bits to direct PADDIR and PADOUT writes; PADOUTSET/PADOUTCLR, interrupt configuration, GPIO enable, lock register, and pad configuration writes remain writable under PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE without pin ownership/privilege guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure GPIO external behavior / gpio_out, gpio_dir, gpio_padcfg, interrupt configuration/state, gpio enable / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester via s_gpio_bus Operation: write/configure GPIO external behavior Object: gpio_out, gpio_dir, gpio_padcfg, interrupt configuration/state, gpio enable Expected guard: privilege/lock/pad-ownership policy for writes affecting external pin drive/direction/output/interrupt behavior Observed behavior: apb_gpio defines registers for PADDIR, PADOUT, INTEN, INTTYPE, GPIOEN, PADCFG, PADOUTSET, PADOUTCLR, GPIOLOCK. Reset clears gpio_out, gpio_dir, gpio_en, gpio_lock and initializes padcfg. On APB writes, PADDIR checks r_gpio_lock[0] and PADOUT checks r_gpio_lock[2], but PADOUTSET and PADOUTCLR update r_gpio_out without checking lock. Interrupt enable/type, GPIO enable, GPIOLOCK, and PADCFG writes are also shown without ownership/privilege guard. Path: shared APB -> s_gpio_bus -> apb_gpio register file Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An APB requester can alter external pin outputs using set/clear paths even if direct output writes are locked, and can modify interrupt and pad configuration state. If any GPIO pins are reserved or security-sensitive, this permits unauthorized external behavior changes.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Top-level mapping of GPIO pins to reserved/security-sensitive functions is not provided. The finding is conditional on GPIO pins affecting protected signals, but the lock-bypass behavior for gpio_out set/clear is explicit.

findings.summary: Clock/reset/power/timer controls observed in apb_soc_ctrl, apb_clkdiv, apb_fll_if, and apb_timer_unit are writable using APB handshake and address decode with no observed privilege, lock, lifecycle, or safe-state checks; FLL lock is not used to block FLL writes.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure clock/reset/power/timing controls / cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, clock divider outputs, FLL control outputs, timer busy/irq/event control / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester Operation: configure clock/reset/power/timing controls Object: cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, clock divider outputs, FLL control outputs, timer busy/irq/event control Expected guard: privilege/lock or safe-state checks for changes that can reset, power down, or destabilize protected domains Observed behavior: apb_soc_ctrl drives cluster reset/power/fetch control outputs from APB-programmed registers with common s_apb_write. apb_clkdiv writes divider registers on PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE and asserts valid pulses. apb_timer_unit writes config, compare, start, reset, and counter-update controls under PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. apb_fll_if write logic decodes direct FLL writes without using lock signals. Path: APB peripheral windows to soc_ctrl, clkdiv, FLL, and timer register files Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: APB software may alter clocks, resets, power, FLL settings, and timers regardless of domain state or privilege, potentially causing denial of service, unsafe state transitions, or lifecycle/security-domain disruption.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Whether these domains are intended protected assets is a policy question; no design security policy was provided. Complete soc_ctrl write cases are truncated, but common update pattern and output fanout are visible.

findings.summary: Interfaces expose DFT/BIST/clock-gating/bypass/mux signal groups. apb_adv_timer and apb_gpio have dft_cg_enable_i inputs. soc_peripherals ties udma dft_cg_enable_i to 1'b0 and passes dft_test_mode_i to uDMA and soc_clk_rst_gen. apb_soc_ctrl exposes cluster_byp_o as an APB-controlled output. A search for 'auth' found no authorization naming. The provided observations do not trace BIST or bbmux/clkgate control sources or prove APB/debug reachability for test controls.
findings.vulnerability_category: enable/override/route test and bypass controls / dft_cg_enable_i, bist_en, bist_pattern, clkgate_* controls, cluster_byp_o, bbmux_* controls / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test/DFT interface, APB/debug requester if connected Operation: enable/override/route test and bypass controls Object: dft_cg_enable_i, bist_en, bist_pattern, clkgate_* controls, cluster_byp_o, bbmux_* controls Expected guard: lifecycle/test-mode authorization and no normal APB/debug assertion of test controls without mediation Observed behavior: Interfaces expose DFT/BIST/clock-gating/bypass/mux signal groups. apb_adv_timer and apb_gpio have dft_cg_enable_i inputs. soc_peripherals ties udma dft_cg_enable_i to 1'b0 and passes dft_test_mode_i to uDMA and soc_clk_rst_gen. apb_soc_ctrl exposes cluster_byp_o as an APB-controlled output. A search for 'auth' found no authorization naming. The provided observations do not trace BIST or bbmux/clkgate control sources or prove APB/debug reachability for test controls. Path: test/DFT inputs and peripheral/control signals to clock-gating, BIST, bypass, and mux logic Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If test or bypass controls are reachable from normal APB/debug paths without lifecycle enable, they could bypass normal operation or disrupt protected clocks/memory/cluster paths. Evidence is insufficient to prove such reachability for most test signals.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Sources and guards for BIST, bbmux, clkgate, and dft_cg_enable_i fanout are not fully traced. Lifecycle/test-mode policy and external test-mode authorization are not provided.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
